prompt:
We're integrating with the aviation authority's drone flight-permit API and I'm done with the half-validated DTOs we have today — a request gets rejected server-side, the operator fixes ONE field, resubmits, gets rejected for the NEXT field, five round trips per permit. New rule for the domain layer: immutable records that validate EVERYTHING up front and report ALL violations in one shot, never fail-fast on the first problem.

Build three sibling .java files (no packages, plain JDK, no build tool — the whole dir runs with the source launcher):

ValidationErrors.java — public class ValidationErrors extends IllegalArgumentException. Constructor (String subject, List<String> violations). getMessage() is exactly subject + ": " + the violations joined with "; ". violations() returns the list — unmodifiable, and a defensive copy (mutating the list passed to the constructor must not leak in).

Geofence.java — public record Geofence(double centerLat, double centerLon, int radiusM, int maxAltitudeM) with a validating compact constructor. Rules, checked in component declaration order, every failure collected before throwing ValidationErrors with subject "Geofence":
- centerLat in [-90, 90] else "centerLat must be between -90.0 and 90.0"
- centerLon in [-180, 180] else "centerLon must be between -180.0 and 180.0"
- radiusM in [50, 5000] else "radiusM must be between 50 and 5000"
- maxAltitudeM in [10, 120] else "maxAltitudeM must be between 10 and 120"
Bounds are inclusive. NaN coordinates are invalid (they're what we get when the GPS fix is garbage — they must trip the same range message, watch your comparison logic). Wither helpers withRadiusM(int) and withMaxAltitudeM(int) return a NEW validated instance with that one component replaced; the original is untouched; withers go through the same validation (bad value = ValidationErrors).

PermitRequest.java — public record PermitRequest(String permitId, String pilotLicense, Geofence zone, LocalDate flightDate, List<String> waivers), same accumulate-then-throw discipline, subject "PermitRequest", messages in component declaration order:
- permitId: null → "permitId must not be null"; otherwise must match DFP- followed by exactly five digits, else "permitId must match DFP-NNNNN"
- pilotLicense: null → "pilotLicense must not be null"; otherwise exactly two uppercase letters + four digits, else "pilotLicense must match LLNNNN"
- zone: null → "zone must not be null"
- flightDate: null → "flightDate must not be null"
- waivers: null → "waivers must not be null"; otherwise per element in index order: a null element → "waivers[i] must not be null", a non-null element that isn't lowercase kebab-case (one or more [a-z0-9]+ groups separated by single hyphens, no leading/trailing hyphen) → "waivers[i] must be lowercase kebab-case" (literal index in place of i); and if any non-null value appears more than once, ONE trailing "waivers must not contain duplicates" after all the element messages.
When the request is valid, store waivers as an unmodifiable defensive copy — callers mutating the list they handed us must not affect the record, and waivers() must reject mutation. Withers: withPilotLicense(String), withZone(Geofence), withFlightDate(LocalDate), withWaivers(List<String>) — new validated instance each, original untouched, records keep value equality (wither round-trips compare equal).

TestMain.java in this directory is the acceptance contract — it pins the exact messages, their order, the accumulation behavior, and the wither semantics. Don't touch it. Done = `java TestMain.java` prints all PASS and exits 0.
